description: _kalshi_get total wall-clock timeout via daemon-thread guard (30s cap). Bounds the slow-drip case where urlopen's per-recv timeout=10 is bypassed by trickle responses, restoring inner-deadline effectiveness in snapshot_universe. Brief was re-scoped mid-session: original directive listed snapshot_universe as out-of-scope based on counter-absent inference, but Phase 0 evidence (bot.log lines 53934/54338/54349) showed today's gap WAS snapshot_universe with the outer wait_for failing silently. Layer 1 only; Layer 2 (outer wait_for binding fix) deferred to Session 102 with watch-list trigger.

metrics.name: longest SCAN CYCLE gap in last 24h during awake hours
metrics.expectation: < 60 min after Session 101. Pre-ship: 1h 59min (13:22-15:21 EDT) AND 2h 6min (15:21-17:28 EDT) on 2026-05-11 — two ~2h gaps in 4h. Post-ship expect inner deadline (300s) to bound to ~5min normal, ~5-10min worst-case. If gaps still > 60min within 14d, the Layer 1 daemon-thread guard isn't bounding the leak — investigate per the watch-list.
metrics.current_value: Post-restart longest 38 min 16s (3 consecutive scans, 2 intervals); 24h-rolling still 2h 6min from pre-restart 15:21-17:28 EDT gap until it ages out (~2026-05-12 17:30 EDT). Cadence regression eliminated.

metrics.name: median SCAN CYCLE interval during awake hours
metrics.expectation: approx 30 min (config IDLE = 1800s) with ≤30s overshoot per Step 9 wall-clock sleep tolerance. Pre-ship median was severely degraded by the multi-hour gaps. Post-ship target: ≤ 35 min median across rolling 24h awake-hours window.
metrics.current_value: Post-restart median 38 min 07s (n=2 intervals: 38:16, 37:58). Slightly above the 35 min target but far below the 60 min watch threshold. The 3-8 min overshoot above the 30 min IDLE config likely reflects Step 3b-8 sync work + wall-clock-sleep polling overshoot; worth a brief audit in a future session if it persists beyond Layer 1's normal noise floor.

metrics.name: count of '_kalshi_get total wall-clock timeout' log entries in last 24h
metrics.expectation: 0-10 under normal Kalshi health; non-zero confirms the new daemon-thread guard is firing on individual hung calls. Higher counts during degradation events are expected and a feature (the guard is doing its job). If sustained > 50/day, Kalshi is severely degraded — flag in the next session brief.

metrics.name: Layer 2 watch-list — outer wait_for binding fix (Session 102 candidate)

Open Session 102 with the asyncio.get_event_loop() -> asyncio.get_running_loop() migration on Python 3.14 (deprecation hypothesis) plus a regression test for the actual production binding scenario.

description: Post-event reversion substrate (Outcome A-defensive): SCAN_INTERVAL_IDLE 1800s→900s for 2x denser non-live universe coverage. Conservative single-knob change; SCAN_INTERVAL_LIVE and SCAN_INTERVAL_PREGAME unchanged. Opens a 30-day re-measurement window: at 2026-06-11 OR when N>=50 observable >=5c events accumulate on KXCOPPERD+KXAAAGASD post-restart, re-run the S106 v1 heuristic measurement and decide promote vs. kill. Investigation 4 returned 16.6% reversion at 1c threshold on N=205 events with reversion decreasing as move size grew (2c=13.5%, 3c=12.3%); KXCOPPERD 33% (N=12) and KXAAAGASD 29% (N=21) were the borderline-evidence subfamilies that activated A-defensive instead of strict Outcome C.

description: Rich live_momentum match-phase state (Outcome B — design doc filed). Phase 0 probing confirmed that ESPN exposes full cricket linescore schema (over_count, innings, wickets, runs) via cricket/8048/scoreboard for IPL, but the bot's ESPN_SPORT_PATHS at bot/config.py:765-771 contains only {nba, mlb, nhl, nfl, ncaab} — IPL is not currently fetched. Enabling it would add a per-tick HTTP fetch the bot has never made, violating S96/S107 'no new network calls' and the S110 brief's explicit 'no ESPN integration, no cricket API' rule. The integration is deferred to a focused follow-on session per docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-12-live-momentum-rich-match-phase-design.md. Tennis (ATP/WTA, disabled per S97) and UFC also out of source. NHL state is reachable but regime._MATCH_PHASE_SPORTS excludes it — separate session. No bot code changes shipped.

The S107 follow-on entry in CLAUDE.md Open Loops remains the durable pointer until the integration ships.

description: consolidator perf fix: hoist archive walk out of compute_active_observations. Pre-fix per-observation re-walk of N observations × 14 gzipped archives = ~670K JSON parses per snapshot, scaling linearly with active observation count (S110 was tipping point). Post-fix walks archives once over [earliest_shipped, now), pre-parses ts, then filters per-observation. Walk count drops from N to 1; runtime 1.88-1.94s → 0.30-0.33s (6.5x). Restored 1640/0 baseline. Zero behavior change. S78 anti-pattern respected (assertion unchanged, fix is structural).

metrics.name: test_consolidator_completes_in_under_2s runtime
metrics.expectation: <1s sustained across runs (post-fix range was 0.30-0.33s, ample headroom under the 2s budget). If runtime drifts above 1.5s, a new structural regression has been introduced (likely a new per-observation work loop that bypassed the S111 hoist). Re-profile compute_active_observations and check whether new ships have re-introduced the per-obs archive walk pattern.
metrics.current_value: 0.30-0.33s (5 consecutive post-fix runs)

metrics.name: compute_active_observations archive-walk count per snapshot (S111 invariant)
metrics.expectation: Exactly 1 walk per snapshot, regardless of len(active_observations). Add an assertion or log if the walk count exceeds 1 — that means a future change re-introduced per-observation iteration. S111's structural fix relies on this invariant.
metrics.current_value: 1 walk per snapshot (post-S111 design)

description: Ship IPL ESPN integration (S107 follow-on resolved). Outcome A — operator-approved relaxation of the S96/S107 'no new network calls' rule scoped to IPL only. Added "ipl": "cricket/8048" to ESPN_SPORT_PATHS; extended _fetch_espn_score with an IPL-only extraction block for over_count / innings / wickets / runs_scored from the current-batting linescore (isBatting AND isCurrent); threaded those fields through _build_live_momentum_decision_context onto decisions.jsonl.extra; _context_match_phase now delegates to bot.regime._match_phase for IPL so the canonical powerplay / middle / death buckets stay single-sourced at regime.py:236-244. Spillover regression-locked at parser-layer (NBA fixture) and context-layer (NBA helper). 14 new tests; pytest 1654/0. CLAUDE.md now carries a Network Call Policy section that explicitly bounds this relaxation to IPL — tennis, UFC, NHL match_phase, and future sport additions still require their own approved exception.

metrics.name: ESPN cricket call success rate during live IPL windows
metrics.expectation: ESPN fetches for sport=ipl return non-empty result on >=90% of attempts during a live IPL match window. Tail bot.log for the 'ESPN fetch OK (ticker=KXIPLGAME-... sport=ipl)' line; absence of that line during a live IPL window for >2 consecutive ticks is the failure signature. If <90% over a match window, investigate ESPN endpoint stability (likely league ID rotation — Phase 0 re-probe required before any fix).
metrics.current_value: TBD (no live IPL window post-restart at ship time)

metrics.name: IPL decisions.jsonl rows carrying over_count during live IPL windows
metrics.expectation: >=80% of IPL rows during live IPL match windows carry an integer over_count in extra. Spot-check via: `tail -200 bot/state/decisions.jsonl | python3 -c "import sys, json; [print(r['ticker'], r['extra'].get('over_count'), r['extra'].get('match_phase')) for line in sys.stdin if (r := json.loads(line)).get('ticker','').startswith('KXIPLGAME')]"`. If <80%, investigate parser team-name matching (Kalshi ticker suffix vs ESPN displayName). Off-season IPL rows are expected to have over_count absent and that is not a failure.
metrics.current_value: TBD (forward-only; no IPL rows with over_count pre-S112)

metrics.name: Cricket-field spillover onto non-IPL decisions
metrics.expectation: Zero non-IPL rows carry over_count / innings / wickets / runs_scored in extra. Spot-check via: `tail -500 bot/state/decisions.jsonl | python3 -c "import sys, json; [print('SPILLOVER:', r['ticker']) for line in sys.stdin if (r := json.loads(line)) and not r.get('ticker','').startswith('KXIPLGAME') and 'over_count' in r.get('extra', {})]"`. Any non-zero output means the cricket branch leaked into another sport's path — revert immediately and investigate. Parser-layer test_fetch_espn_score_nba_unchanged_shape + context-layer test_live_momentum_context_helper_nba_no_cricket_spillover regression-lock this at unit-test time.
metrics.current_value: 0 (regression-locked)

metrics.name: 14d cohort re-measurement (fires 2026-05-25 OR N>=20 IPL rows with over_count populated)
metrics.expectation: At 2026-05-25 OR when >=20 IPL decisions.jsonl rows have carried over_count, re-measure (a) ESPN cricket call success rate during the observed live IPL windows; (b) match_phase distribution across powerplay / middle / death; (c) whether IPL cohort-N × match_phase × leader_strength buckets are now thick enough for the S102 IPL-profitability analysis to slice by phase. If (a) <90% or (c) every match_phase bucket remains N<5, surface as a follow-on operational-hygiene candidate.
metrics.current_value: S115 (2026-05-12) measurement: 0 IPL live_momentum decisions post-S112 ship (22:00Z cutoff). All 11 IPL rows in decisions.jsonl are pre-cutoff (2026-05-10T16:19 → 2026-05-11T17:27). Bot is actively logging post-cutoff for non-IPL sports; no live IPL match window since ship. Joint per-IPL-phase analysis with S107 IPL slice blocked until window arrives. Re-measure 2026-05-25 OR at next live IPL window.

description: LIVE_GAME_CARDS_ENABLED flag (default False) gates send_message_get_id + edit_message_by_id in bot/notifier.py. Per-tick editMessageText calls suppressed; initial-card sendMessage suppressed. Operator doesn't read Telegram so per-tick API calls update a status card nobody watches — the disable removes that cost. Event-driven Telegram paths (send_confirmation, send_resolution, send_daily_summary, slash commands) are preserved — they route through send_message() and the base _telegram_call() machinery, not the two disabled methods. Cascade design: send_message_get_id returns None when flag is False, so LiveGameWatcher.status_msg_id stays None, so the existing 'if self.status_msg_id:' guards at live_watcher.py:2205 / 2633 short-circuit per-tick edits without any caller-side changes. 4 new pytest cases (2 no-op assertions, 1 flag=True regression lock, 1 send_message preservation). 3 existing edit_message_by_id tests updated to monkeypatch LIVE_GAME_CARDS_ENABLED=True so they continue exercising the dedup + throttle machinery for the case where the operator flips the flag back on. pytest 1654 → 1658 / 0 failures. Restart 63214 → 85470 via launchctl, cwd-verified.

metrics.name: Telegram editMessageText / edit_message_by_id call volume per 24h
metrics.expectation: 0 with flag False. Verify via `grep -c "edit_message_text\|edit_message_by_id" bot/logs/bot.log` against the rolling log. Any non-zero hit is either (a) someone flipped the flag back on (intentional re-enable), or (b) a path that bypasses the notifier-side guards — investigate.
metrics.current_value: 0 — verified at ship time over the first 3 min post-restart with 3 active LiveGameWatcher MOMENTUM instances (oklahoma, minnesota, casper ruud) firing per-tick logic normally and the cascade short-circuiting cleanly

metrics.name: Telegram 'Message is not modified' error count per 24h
metrics.expectation: 0 with flag False (errors only fired when editMessageText fired, so calls=0 implies errors=0). Pre-restart baseline was ~0.6/hour in bot/logs/bot.log.1 (Phase 0 measured 42 raw lines / 70.3h window). NOT the 236/hour the CLAUDE.md Open Loops entry framed — that framing was wrong by ~three orders of magnitude. Do not claim the dedup logic was 'fixed' — it was bypassed; the bug at bot/notifier.py:993 (hash store only after success) is unchanged.
metrics.current_value: S115 (2026-05-12) verified ~5h post-ship: 0 errors in bot/logs/bot.log post-ship (rate 0.00/hr vs corrected baseline ~0.6/hr). editMessageText calls continued 12 min post-ship (236 calls 22:54:14→23:05:59, all HTTP 200) then ceased fully — consistent with flag taking effect on next live-card render cycle. No residual call site to chase. HEALTHY.

metrics.name: Event-driven Telegram path liveness (send_confirmation / send_resolution / send_daily_summary / slash commands)
metrics.expectation: These continue firing unaffected. test_send_message_preserved_when_live_cards_disabled regression-locks this at unit-test time. Operator-discretionary spot check: send `/status` over Telegram and confirm response. Note: operator-discretionary because the operator's stated preference is to not read Telegram, but if these paths ever need to fire (e.g. a real-mode resolution alert), they MUST work regardless of the flag.
metrics.current_value: regression-locked at unit-test time (test_send_message_preserved_when_live_cards_disabled); operator-discretionary spot check not performed at ship

metrics.name: 14d re-evaluation trigger
metrics.expectation: At 2026-05-26 OR if any metric above drifts non-zero before then, re-evaluate: (a) confirm the disable is still appropriate (operator hasn't changed Telegram-usage stance); (b) decide whether to remove the live-card code entirely (currently kept reversible) vs. fix the underlying dedup bug at bot/notifier.py:993 vs. leave as-is. If operator wants live cards back, flipping LIVE_GAME_CARDS_ENABLED=True is the entire change — no other code paths assumed False.
metrics.current_value: not yet fired (S113 ship time)

description: S105 cross-platform Kalshi↔Polymarket validation corpus scraping + candidate-generation infrastructure shipped via tools/build_cross_platform_corpus.py (470-line single-file CLI). Scrapes Kalshi /events?status=settled&with_nested_markets=true (politics/elections/economics/crypto/world, filtered client-side because the API ignores its own `category` param) + Polymarket Gamma /markets?closed=true&order=closedTime&ascending=false (no category filter — Polymarket no longer tags recent markets, rely on token-set Jaccard text similarity at pair-time). Live 3-day scrape: 735K unique Kalshi × 97K Polymarket → 604,603 candidate pairs (2,937 suggested-MATCH at Jaccard ≥0.60, 601,666 NEEDS_REVIEW at Jaccard 0.40–0.60). Queue capped at top 5,000 by Jaccard descending (committable 3.3 MB) for operator hand-labeling; full 604K corpus preserved at bot/state/cache/cross_platform_candidate_pairs_full.jsonl (gitignored). Two API quirks caught mid-implementation and regression-locked: Kalshi 5× duplication via category-param iteration (fixed: single-pass + client-side filter + ticker dedupe); Polymarket recent markets lack top-level category field (fixed: category filter removed). 49 pytest cases, no agent/ deps, no scanner/executor/strategy changes, no bot restart. Full pytest 1707/0 (1658 baseline + 49 net). Operator labeling protocol documented in CLAUDE.md Cross-platform corpus labeling protocol section.

metrics.name: operator_pairs_labeled
metrics.expectation: ≥40 within 30 days (≥20 MATCH + ≥20 NO_MATCH per S105 spec). Operator opens bot/state/cross_platform_labeling_queue.jsonl and sets operator_label to 'MATCH' / 'NEEDS_REVIEW' / 'NO_MATCH' on each row. Suggested-label hint is non-authoritative. Expected ~30-90 sec per pair. Target throughput ≥40 labels per 60-90 min session. Per-rung Bitcoin / Ethereum / sports / esports MATCH suggestions are well-calibrated by spot-check but are NOT S105's politics/econ target use case — operator should label honestly; the downstream matcher's scope filter will exclude sports/esports.
metrics.current_value: 0 operator labels; 40 Codex-labeled disagreement pairs from S118 pending Claude/operator spot-check

metrics.name: matcher_implementation_unblocked
metrics.expectation: When ≥20 labeled MATCH + ≥20 labeled NO_MATCH accumulate in cross_platform_labeling_queue.jsonl, the S105 matcher implementation session can proceed. The matcher must achieve zero false positives on labeled-MATCH pairs and ≥30% high-confidence recovery (per S105 spec).
metrics.current_value: partially_unblocked_by_codex_conflict_slice; balanced MATCH recall labels still pending

metrics.name: 30d re-evaluation trigger
metrics.expectation: At 2026-06-11 OR when operator_pairs_labeled reaches 40, re-evaluate. (a) If labeled count is ≥40, unblock matcher implementation session (separate from S116). (b) If labeled count is <20 AND corpus quality is the bottleneck, operator should re-run with wider lookback (`python3 tools/build_cross_platform_corpus.py --lookback-days 30`) to surface politics/econ pairs that settle slower than the dominant per-game / per-hour-strike inventory. (c) If labeled count is <20 AND operator workflow is the bottleneck, ship the deferred tools/label_cross_platform_pair.py CLI helper (next/skip/label/quit) before continuing.

description: S105 cross-platform Kalshi↔Polymarket matcher v1 + validation harness shipped as unvalidated infrastructure. bot/cross_platform_matcher.py is pure deterministic code (MatchResult enum, ±24h date alignment, token-set Jaccard, normalized resolution-source upgrade, empty ticker-family rules, manual override precedence, resolved-outcome conflict rule, insufficient-data handling). tools/validate_cross_platform_matcher.py runs the matcher over the S116 5,000-row queue and writes top matcher-vs-heuristic disagreements to bot/state/matcher_heuristic_disagreement_pairs.jsonl. Live S117 harness run: MATCH_HIGH_CONFIDENCE=2,937; MATCH_NEEDS_REVIEW=963; NO_MATCH=1,100; INSUFFICIENT_DATA=0; heuristic agreement 3,900/5,000 (78.0%); all 1,100 disagreements are NEEDS_REVIEW -> NO_MATCH due to resolved_outcome_conflict. Operator labels remain 0, so validation is pending operator review and no accuracy claim is made. 20 new tests; full pytest 1727/0. No labels edited, no LLM matching, no scanner/executor/strategy wiring, no bot restart.

description: Codex-direction MATCH validation. Stratified-sampled 40 unlabeled MATCH_HIGH_CONFIDENCE pairs from the matcher's 2,937 positive classifications (20 boundary = lowest matcher Jaccard, 20 interior = random from rest, seed=42) and labeled them via an independent labeler with labeler=codex, 2-3 sentence reasoning, and mechanical outcome cross-check. Outcome B: matcher v1 has structural false positives. Harness on --labeler codex (80 combined S118+S119 labels): accuracy 67.5%, false_positives=24, false_negatives=0. The hard-rule override (DIVERGED outcomes + Codex-MATCH -> NO_MATCH) never fired because the matcher's upstream filter at bot/cross_platform_matcher.py:207-214 guarantees converged outcomes on MATCH_HIGH_CONFIDENCE. Matcher tuning queued as S120; no matcher code or bot restart in this session.

metrics.name: codex_labels_combined_s118_s119
metrics.expectation: 80 cross_platform_labeling_queue.jsonl rows carry labeler=codex (40 NO_MATCH from S118 + 14 MATCH + 24 NO_MATCH + 2 NEEDS_REVIEW from S119). All other queue rows remain operator_label=null.
metrics.current_value: 80 labeled (40 S118 + 40 S119); 4,920 remain operator_label=null

metrics.name: outcome_cross_check_distribution_s119
metrics.expectation: S119 samples are drawn from matcher's MATCH_HIGH_CONFIDENCE bucket. Per matcher.py upstream filter, all 40 must have converged outcomes (BOTH_YES or BOTH_NO); DIVERGED on a HIGH_CONFIDENCE classification would itself be a critical matcher bug.
metrics.current_value: BOTH_YES=18; BOTH_NO=22; DIVERGED=0; INSUFFICIENT_DATA=0

metrics.name: matcher_validation_match_direction_on_codex_subset
metrics.expectation: Outcome B baseline: matcher's MATCH_HIGH_CONFIDENCE classifications agree with Codex labels on only 14 / 40 = 35% of S119 samples; 24 are false positives (label=NO_MATCH); 2 are over-confident (label=NEEDS_REVIEW). Combined with S118: 80 labels, accuracy 67.5%, false_positives=24, false_negatives=0. S120 tuning must reduce false_positives toward 0 without regressing the S118 NO_MATCH-direction validation.
metrics.current_value: S119 alone: accuracy=35%; FP=24; FN=0. Combined (S118+S119): accuracy=67.5%; FP=24; FN=0; confusion={NO_MATCH->no_match: 40, NO_MATCH->match_high_confidence: 24, MATCH->match_high_confidence: 14, NEEDS_REVIEW->match_high_confidence: 2}

metrics.name: per_stratum_false_positive_rate_s119
metrics.expectation: Boundary-20 (lowest matcher Jaccard = 0.60 threshold floor) is the load-bearing stress test. Interior-20 covers the bucket interior. If boundary FP rate >> interior FP rate, threshold tuning is the right S120 lever; if both are similar, the matcher needs a richer features (bet-type / settlement-window discrimination).
metrics.current_value: boundary: 17 NO_MATCH + 1 NEEDS_REVIEW + 2 MATCH = 18/20 = 90% wrong (85% strict FP). interior: 7 NO_MATCH + 1 NEEDS_REVIEW + 12 MATCH = 8/20 = 40% wrong (35% strict FP).

metrics.name: false_positive_failure_pattern_s119
metrics.expectation: The matcher's failures are STRUCTURAL, not threshold-tunable alone. Two failure modes: (1) same-fixture different-bet-type (match-winner vs O/U vs exact-score vs map-handicap vs set 1/set 2 winner; ~20 of 24 boundary FPs); (2) crypto hourly snapshot (Kalshi 'at 4pm EDT') vs end-of-day window (Polymarket 'on May 10'; ~4 interior FPs). S120 must address bet-type discrimination AND settlement-window granularity, not just raise the Jaccard threshold.
metrics.current_value: 20 FPs in 'same fixture, different bet type' shape; 4 FPs in 'crypto hourly vs end-of-day' shape. 0 FPs from token-overlap-but-different-event (the matcher correctly handles that via outcome divergence)

metrics.name: hard_rule_override_fire_count_s119
metrics.expectation: The hard rule (DIVERGED outcomes + Codex-MATCH -> NO_MATCH) is belt-and-suspenders for matcher bugs. Per bot/cross_platform_matcher.py:207-214 the matcher REJECTS DIVERGED outcomes upstream before classifying HIGH_CONFIDENCE, so this rule should never fire on a S119 sample. A non-zero count indicates a matcher regression that S120 must investigate.
metrics.current_value: 0 (as expected)

S120 tuning will both fix the matcher and update this assertion (toward FP=0); the test ratchets in both directions to catch unintended drift.
metrics.current_value: Locked at S119 baseline (FP=24, FN=0); pytest 1729/0

description: Matcher v2 shipped for S105 cross-platform Kalshi-Polymarket settlement matching. bot/cross_platform_matcher.py now extracts deterministic bet-type signatures and explicit time-granularity markers before applying the existing Jaccard/source/family rules. S120 validation on the 80 Codex-labeled S118+S119 corpus: safety accuracy=97.5%, exact_accuracy=91.25%, false_positives=0, false_negatives=0. Full 5,000-row distribution shifted from S119 baseline MATCH_HIGH_CONFIDENCE=2,937 / MATCH_NEEDS_REVIEW=963 / NO_MATCH=1,100 to MATCH_HIGH_CONFIDENCE=1,590 / MATCH_NEEDS_REVIEW=1,558 / NO_MATCH=1,852. No bot restart; matcher is not live-wired.
